title: Brauer-Zero Dissolution Operator
author: Phan Thành Trung (Trung Bờm) × GPT
purpose: Conditionally dissolve a constraint-locked character state into the ground symmetry / invariance projection when height reaches the irreducible base layer or when the constraint block is invalid.

core_rule.positive_height: h(x,C)>ε ⇒ retain character in block B_C under strict policy

runtime_equations.projection: Π_G(x): state → ground symmetry / invariance representative

safety.statement: BZDO does not dissolve unconditionally. It dissolves only when h≈0 or when the active constraint block is invalid and the policy allows force_symmetry.
safety.invalid_height: If height is NaN, infinite, or negative, runtime retains state for safety.
safety.invalid_state: If validateState(x) fails, runtime returns invalid_retain.

axioms_checked: invalid block forces symmetry projection by default
upgrade_notes: The previous runtime only handled h=0, invalid block, and retain branches with external hooks.
v1.1 adds height tolerance, strict/continuous release policies, trace entries, batch execution, vector reference hooks, self-tests, and O.i intermediate export support.
Π_G and h remain hook-based by design so BZDO can operate across cognition, AI, symbolic states, and numeric vector states.

relations: HZSO drives states toward height-zero symmetry.
BZDO decides whether character should be retained, dissolved, or force-projected.
